paper_title: Microalgae Transformation and Gene Expression Analysis

methods.description: Describes the conditions used for growing batch cultures of the microalga P. tricornutum.

steps.description: P. tricornutum was obtained from the Freshwater Algae Culture Collection, Institute of Hydrobiology, .
steps.searchable_summary: obtain microalgae culture stock

steps.description: The microalgae were grown as batch cultures in Erlenmeyer flasks containing f/2 medium, which was sterilised through 0.22-μm filters (Millipore, Billerica, MA, USA).
steps.searchable_summary: prepare and sterilize f/2 medium for batch culture

steps.description: The cultures were grown at 21±1°C in an artificial climate incubator.
steps.searchable_summary: incubate microalgae cultures at controlled temperature

steps.description: Cool-white fluorescent tubes provided an irradiance of 200 μmol photons m −2 s −1 under long-day light conditions (15/9 h light/dark regime).
steps.searchable_summary: provide controlled light conditions for microalgae culture

methods.method_name: Selection and Subculturing of Transformed Microalgae
methods.description: Procedure for identifying, counting, picking, and propagating putative transformed microalgae colonies on selective media containing chloramphenicol to obtain homoplastomic lines.

steps.description: After 4 weeks of incubation of the plates under standard growth conditions, the putative transformed colonies were counted.
steps.searchable_summary: count transformed microalgae colonies on selection plates

steps.description: The viable colonies were picked and inoculated into the fresh liquid f/2 medium containing 200 mg/l chloramphenicol
steps.searchable_summary: pick viable colonies and inoculate into liquid selection medium

steps.description: and then subjected to five more subculture cycles on selection medium to obtain homoplastomic microalgae.
steps.searchable_summary: perform multiple subculture cycles on selection medium

methods.method_name: Plastid Isolation by Sucrose Gradient Centrifugation
methods.description: Method for isolating plastids from P. tricornutum cells using differential and sucrose gradient centrifugation.

steps.description: Cells in 50 ml culture in the exponential phase were harvested by centrifugation at 5,000g for 10 min at 4°C.
steps.searchable_summary: harvest microalgae cells by centrifugation

steps.description: The algal pellet was ground to a fine powder in liquid nitrogen
steps.searchable_summary: grind cell pellet in liquid nitrogen

steps.description: and transferred to 1.5 ml grinding buffer (0.3 M sucrose, 40 mM Tris-HCl (pH 7.8), 5 mM MgCl 2 , 1 mM PMSF) followed by incubation on ice for 5 min.
steps.searchable_summary: resuspend ground powder in grinding buffer and incubate on ice

steps.description: The homogenate was subsequently separated by centrifugation at 350g for 10 min at 4°C, and the pellet contained the nuclear fraction.
steps.searchable_summary: separate nuclear fraction by low-speed centrifugation

steps.description: The supernatant was carefully transferred to a new tube without disturbing the pellet, followed by centrifugation at 12,000g for 20 min at 4°C, and the resultant pellet predominantly consisted of plastids.
steps.searchable_summary: separate plastid-rich fraction by high-speed centrifugation

steps.description: The plastid-rich fraction was confirmed by observation of chlorophyll fluorescence under a fluorescence microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: confirm plastid fraction using fluorescence microscopy

methods.description: Procedure for extracting DNA specifically from the isolated plastid-rich fraction using a commercial kit.

steps.description: The plastid DNA was extracted from the plastid-rich fraction using the Universal Genomic DNA Extraction Kit Ver.3.0 (TaKaRa).
steps.searchable_summary: extract plastid DNA using commercial kit
inputs: Universal Genomic DNA Extraction Kit Ver.3.0 (TaKaRa)

methods.method_name: PCR for Transgene Confirmation and Integration Validation
methods.description: Using Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR) to confirm the presence of the CAT gene and validate DNA integration/homologous recombination in transformed microalgae.

steps.description: To confirm the presence of the CAT gene in the microalgae, the cells were tested using PCR with the CAT-specific primers P5 and P6.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCR to confirm presence of CAT gene

steps.description: Simultaneously, to validate the DNA integration and homologous recombination between trnA and trnI in the plastid genome in the transformed cells, PCR was performed with the primers IRf1 (5′-ATCGGCTAACTCCGTGCCAG-3′) or IRf1 (5′-TAACTATTTCTTATGACCTTTCC-3′) and CATr (5′-AGCAACTGACTGAAATGCCTC-3′).
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCR to validate DNA integration and homologous recombination

steps.description: The PCR reaction mixture (25 μl) consisted of 250 ng template, 5 pmol of each primer, 0.75 U LA Taq polymerase (TaKaRa), 2.5 μl 10× PCR reaction buffer, 1.5 μl of 25 mM MgCl 2 and 0.25 μl of each 10 mM dNTP.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare PCR reaction mixture

steps.description: PCR amplification was initiated with denaturation at 95°C for 3 min
steps.searchable_summary: perform initial denaturation step in PCR

steps.description: followed by 35 cycles of 95°C 1 min, 58°C 1 min and 72°C 1 min.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCR amplification cycles

methods.description: Procedure for analyzing the expected PCR band by purification from a gel and subsequent sequencing.

steps.description: The expected PCR band on the gel was purified
steps.searchable_summary: purify specific PCR band from gel

steps.description: and analysed by sequencing.
steps.searchable_summary: sequence purified DNA fragment

methods.description: Procedure to further confirm gene integration by Southern blot analysis.

steps.description: The Universal Genomic DNA Extraction Kit Ver. 3.0 (TaKaRa) was used to extract the genomic DNA from the pPtc-eGFP transformed and untransformed control cells.
steps.searchable_summary: extract genomic DNA from transformed and control cells
inputs: Universal Genomic DNA Extraction Kit Ver. 3.0 (TaKaRa)

steps.description: Samples containing 5 μg of genomic DNA were digested with EcoRI.
steps.searchable_summary: digest genomic DNA with restriction enzyme EcoRI

steps.description: The EcoRI/XbaI fragment in the coding region of CAT (Fig. 1) was used to prepare a DIG-labelled probe.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare DIG-labelled DNA probe
inputs: DIG DNA Labeling and Detection Kit (Roche, USA)

steps.description: Southern blot analysis was carried out to further confirm the gene integration as described previously ) by using the DIG DNA Labeling and Detection Kit (Roche, USA) following the manufacturer's instructions.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Southern blot analysis for gene integration confirmation

methods.method_name: Total RNA Extraction and cDNA Synthesis
methods.description: Procedure for extracting total RNA from microalgae cells and synthesizing complementary DNA (cDNA) for gene expression analysis.

steps.description: For quantification of the transcripts, the RNeasy Plant Mini kit (QIAGEN, USA) was used to extract the total RNA from 1× 10 8 pPtc-eGFP transformed and untransformed P. tricornutum cells.
steps.searchable_summary: extract total RNA from transformed and control cells using kit

steps.description: During extraction, the RNA samples were treated with QIAGEN RNase-free DNase I to remove residualcontaminating DNA.
steps.searchable_summary: perform DNase treatment on RNA samples

steps.description: In total, 2 μg of total RNA was reverse transcribed with random hexamer primers using an Omniscript reverse transcription kit (QIAGEN).
steps.searchable_summary: synthesize cDNA from total RNA using reverse transcription kit

methods.method_name: Real-Time PCR (qPCR) for Gene Expression Quantification
methods.description: Using quantitative Real-Time PCR (qPCR) to measure the relative transcription levels of target genes (CAT, eGFP) normalized to a housekeeping gene (β-actin).

steps.description: The primers for CAT, eGFP and β-actin housekeeping marker were designed according to previous reports (De Riso et al. 2009;Niu et al. 2012).
steps.searchable_summary: design or obtain primers for target and housekeeping genes

steps.description: The qPCR was conducted in 96-well optical reaction plates using a SYBR Green Kit (Ruizhen Co., China) according to the manufacturer's instructions.
steps.searchable_summary: set up and run qPCR reaction using SYBR Green kit

steps.description: Each sample was amplified in triplicate on a 7300 Sequence Detection System (Applied Biosystems, USA).
steps.searchable_summary: run qPCR samples in technical replicates

steps.description: The threshold cycle for each well was measured
steps.searchable_summary: measure threshold cycle (Ct) from qPCR data

steps.description: and the relative transcription levels of the target gene compared with the control were quantified after normalisation to β-actin.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate relative gene expression levels using Ct values and normalization
inputs: Ct values (target genes, housekeeping gene, control samples)

methods.description: Procedure for extracting total protein from P. tricornutum cells using a commercial kit.

steps.description: a protein extraction kit (KeyGEN, Nanjin, China) was used to extract the total protein from 1×10 8 P. tricornutum cells transformed with pPtc-eGFP and an untransformed control.
steps.searchable_summary: extract total protein from transformed and control cells using kit

methods.description: Method for measuring the concentration of extracted total protein using the Bicinchoninic Acid (BCA) assay.

steps.description: The protein concentrations were measured by the bicinchoninic acid assay (BCA assay).
steps.searchable_summary: measure protein concentration using BCA assay

methods.description: Procedure for detecting specific proteins (eGFP, Myc-tagged polypeptide) using antibodies after separation by SDS-PAGE and transfer to a membrane.

steps.description: Twenty micrograms of protein from each sample was resolved by 12 % SDS-PAGE at 100 V for 100 min
steps.searchable_summary: perform SDS-PAGE to separate proteins
outputs: Proteins separated by size in gel

steps.description: and stained with Coomassie Brilliant Blue G-250 to visualise the protein bands.
steps.searchable_summary: stain SDS-PAGE gel with Coomassie blue

steps.description: Two other identical gels were electrotransferred to a PVDF membrane.
steps.searchable_summary: electrotransfer proteins from gel to PVDF membrane

steps.description: The membranes were blocked in phosphatebuffered saline and 0.1 % Tween 20 (PBST) (137 mM NaCl, 2.7 mM KCl, 10 mM Na 2 HPO 4 , 1.8 mM KH 2 PO 4 , 0.5 % Tween 20, pH 7.6) containing 5 % bovine serum albumin (BSA) for 2 h at room temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: block Western blot membrane with BSA in PBST

steps.description: After washing three times in PBST, the membranes were incubated with an anti-Myc antibody (Invitrogen, USA) or an anti-GFP antibody (Epitomics, USA) at a 1:5,000 dilution in 5 % BSA in PBST for 2 h at room temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: incubate membrane with primary antibodies (anti-Myc, anti-GFP)

steps.description: The anti-GAPDH and anti-βactin antibodies (Abcam, UK) were used as internal controls at a dilution of 1:5,000.
steps.searchable_summary: incubate membrane with internal control primary antibodies (anti-GAPDH, anti-beta-actin)
outputs: Membrane with internal control primary antibodies bound

steps.description: The membranes were then washed three times with PBST
steps.searchable_summary: wash membrane after primary antibody incubation

steps.description: and incubated with a horseradish peroxidase (HRP)-conjugated secondary antibody (Kangwei, China) at a 1:5,000 dilution in PBST for 1 h at room temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: incubate membrane with HRP-conjugated secondary antibody

steps.description: The blots were washed three times
steps.searchable_summary: wash membrane after secondary antibody incubation

steps.description: and developed with tetramethyl-benzidine (TMB) reagent (Beyotime, China).
steps.searchable_summary: develop Western blot using TMB reagent
